text.title: Web App Manifest, Workbox Caching Strategies, SW Registration
text.summary: Required manifest fields for installability, Workbox caching strategy selection per resource type, and service worker registration with update detection.

section.title: Web App Manifest Requirements

rule.statement: Manifest must include name, short_name, start_url, display: standalone, and icon entries for 192px and 512px with a maskable variant for Android adaptive icons.
rule.rationale: Missing any of these fields fails the installability check. The 512px maskable icon is required for the Android adaptive icon system; without it, install is blocked on some devices.

section.reference: Complete Web App Manifest with required fields and icon sizes

rule.statement: Use NetworkFirst for API responses and HTML navigation; CacheFirst for images and fonts; StaleWhileRevalidate for JS/CSS static assets.
rule.rationale: CacheFirst on APIs returns stale data indefinitely. NetworkFirst with a networkTimeoutSeconds fallback gives fresh data when online and cached data when offline.

example.code: // WRONG - CacheFirst on API returns stale data indefinitely registerRoute(({ url }) => url.pathname.startsWith('/api/'), new CacheFirst());

rule.statement: Register the SW from the same origin as the app; handle updatefound events to notify users of new versions rather than silently calling skipWaiting.
rule.rationale: Silent skipWaiting terminates in-flight requests and can corrupt client state mid-session. An explicit user-driven reload preserves UX.

rule.statement: Pre-cache and serve /offline.html from the SW install event; register it as the fallback for all failed navigations.
rule.rationale: Without an offline fallback, failed navigations show a browser error page — the service worker makes the experience worse than having no SW at all.
